mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and housing starts indicates synchronized real-economy weakness that compresses corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 2.5% year-over-year, a subsequent rise in the federal funds rate accelerates discount rate growth faster than earnings can adjust, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when both CPI exceeds 2.5% and FEDFUNDS rises, or the signal's predictive power vanishes in low-inflation regimes despite the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity that supports corporate revenue trajectories, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with payroll growth streaks over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP growth below 1.0% signals an approaching or confirmed economic contraction, prompting institutional de-risking that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in the majority of quarters where GDP growth falls below 1.0%, or the signal's information ratio drops below zero over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment rises concurrently with a drop in real consumer spending (RSAFS), it confirms a broad-based demand shock that erodes profit margins and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight monetary policy where the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0%, a sustained drop in industrial production accelerates supply-side constraints that trigger stagflation fears, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when industrial production falls during periods where the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year backtest.
